banana.description: High-value fruit crop suited for tropical and subtropical regions. Requires well-drained fertile soil and high humidity.

barley.description: Hardy cereal crop tolerant of poor soils. Used for food, malt, and animal feed. Grows well in cooler regions.

chickpea.description: A key pulse crop that fixes atmospheric nitrogen. Drought-tolerant and suitable for rainfed conditions.

cotton.description: Major cash crop best suited for black cotton soil (regur). Requires warm climate with moderate rainfall.

garlic.description: High-value bulb crop. Prefers cool weather during bulb development. Well-drained loamy soils produce best quality.

groundnut.description: Important oilseed and food crop. Requires sandy loam or red soil with good drainage.

jute.description: Natural fiber crop requiring hot and humid climate. Best grown in alluvial soil of West Bengal and Assam.

lentil.description: High-protein pulse crop best in cool, dry weather. Fixes nitrogen and improves soil health.

maize.description: Versatile cereal crop used for food, fodder, and industrial purposes. Grows well in diverse climatic conditions.

mango.description: King of fruits — requires distinct dry and wet seasons. Deep, well-drained alluvial or loamy soils are ideal.

moong.description: Short-duration summer pulse rich in protein. Well-suited for sandy loam soils with moderate rainfall.

mustard.description: Important oilseed crop for northern India. Requires cool, dry weather during growth and warm conditions for maturity.

onion.description: Essential vegetable crop with strong market demand. Requires moderate climate and well-drained loamy soil.

pigeonpea.description: Drought-tolerant perennial/annual pulse. Widely grown in peninsular India on various soil types.

potato.description: Cool-season vegetable crop. India is the second-largest producer. Requires well-drained, loose, sandy loam soil.

rice.description: A staple cereal crop ideal for warm, humid conditions with heavy irrigation. Grows best in paddy fields with clay or loamy soil.

sorghum.description: Drought-tolerant cereal ideal for dryland farming. Good for semi-arid regions with limited water supply.

soybean.description: High-protein oilseed crop. Major crop in Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra. Suitable for black and red soils.

sugarcane.description: Long-duration commercial crop used for sugar production. Requires tropical climate and heavy irrigation.

sunflower.description: Oilseed crop with high yield and adaptability. Tolerant of varied soils, high demand for edible oil.

tomato.description: Major vegetable crop grown across India. Requires moderate climate and well-drained fertile soil.

wheat.description: India's primary Rabi crop. Requires cool growing conditions and moderate water. Ideal for Indo-Gangetic plains.
